rules.rule: Method names must contain a verb describing the action performed.
Async methods returning Task or ValueTask must be postfixed with Async.
Input parameter names must reflect the specific property — not generic names like text, name, or id without entity prefix.
Parameters representing an entity field must include the entity name (e.g., studentId, not id).
Use named parameter aliases when the variable name does not fully match the parameter name. Never pass unnamed literal values.
Methods with exactly one line of logic must use fat-arrow (=>) syntax.
A fat-arrow one-liner exceeding 120 characters must break after => and indent continuation with one extra tab.
Methods with multiple lines of logic must use a scoped block { }. Must not use fat-arrow for multi-liner logic.
In multi-liner methods, the final return statement must be preceded by one blank line.
Two consecutive non-return calls under 120 chars may stack. A multi-line call must be separated from the next by a blank line.
Method declarations exceeding 120 characters must break parameter list to next line with one parameter per line.
Method chains must use uglification: first call on invocation line, each subsequent call indented one additional tab.
